plan_metadata.name: Teams Agent With Data AI Search Azure OpenAI py Local Debug

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option from the dropdown menu in the "New Project" window.
Click the "General Teams Agent" dropdown option in the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" menu.
Click the "Azure AI Search" option in the "Select an option to load your data" dropdown within the "Teams Agent with Data" window of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Python" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the directory for the project location (`/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Click the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the ".env.local.user" file in the "env" folder within the VS Code Explorer sidebar to select or open it.
Click in the code editor of VS Code at line 10, column 1 within the .env.local.user file to place the text cursor at the start of an empty line for editing environment variables.
Cut the currently selected line (line 10) in the .env.local.user file within Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+X.
Click the ".env.playground.user" file in the "env" folder within the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to select or open it.
Click inside the editor pane of Visual Studio Code, specifically within the opened ".env.playground.user" file at line 8, to place the text cursor after the "AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT" value.
Cut the selected text in the ".env.playground.user" file at line 10 using Ctrl+X in Visual Studio Code.
Click the insertion point in the VS Code editor on line 10, immediately after "SECRET_AZURE_SEARCH_KEY=", within the .env.playground.user file.
Type #text:"text-embedding-ada-002" at the end of the AZURE_OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DEPLOYMENT field in the .env.playground.user file within the VS Code editor.
Press the Down Arrow key to move the cursor from line 9 to line 10 in the .env.playground.user file within Visual Studio Code.
Type ${{env:AZURE_SEARCH_KEY}} at the end of line 10, after 'SECRET_AZURE_SEARCH_KEY=' in the .env.playground.user file within Visual Studio Code.
Press the Down Arrow key to move the cursor from line 10 (SECRET_AZURE_SEARCH_KEY) to line 11 (AZURE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT) in the .env.playground.user file within Visual Studio Code.
Type ${{env:AZURE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT}} at the cursor position in the .env.playground.user file opened in Visual Studio Code, specifically after 'AZURE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT=' on line 11.
Save changes in the ".env.playground.user" file by pressing Ctrl+S in Visual Studio Code.
Right-click on the tab for the file ".env.playground.user" in the Visual Studio Code editor to open the context menu for tab actions.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu to close the selected file tab in Visual Studio Code.
